data.description:
## Why
Specialist overlays are where domain expertise belongs. Per the locked principle, each overlay carries
BOTH halves — recall patterns and FP-discrimination — so the generic Pass-2 verifier stays domain-blind.

## What
Enrich six overlays with dso content (tests first to prove the pattern, then the five):
- tests: full behavioral-testing standard — recall (change-detector, tautological, over/under-mocking,
  source-grepping, existence-only) AND FP-guards (Four-Criterion test, Rule-5 structural-artifact
  exception, the VALID-patterns-not-to-flag). NOT-flag base rules do not apply to test-correctness.
- security: dso's 8 red-team concerns + hard exclusions deferring to the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria
  by name + rationalizations-to-reject.
- performance: 8 AI-advantaged concerns; bright-line reasoning captured as evidence for Pass-2.
- api-compat: asymmetric-change / producer-consumer drift.
- db-migrations: delete+replace symmetry, destructive/backfill/lock-duration.
- llm-prompts: ui-artifact + domain-mismatch + contract-drift for rebar's own prompt seam.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-{tests,security,performance,api-compat,db-migrations,llm-prompts}.md.
Each task lands with its overlay's eval (recall + no-fire).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each of the six overlays enriched with both recall patterns and FP-guards.
- [ ] Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.
- [ ] Overlays remain advisory; Pass-2 verifier receives no domain-specific rubric.
data.title: Specialist overlay enrichment (tests + five)
